# Invalidation

Invalidation is the center of the cache design, not an afterthought.

HydraCache starts with explicit invalidation:

- values may have tags
- tags map back to cache keys
- callers invalidate tags after writes
- TTL bounds the damage when invalidation is missed

```rust
cache.invalidate_tag("user:42").await?;
cache.invalidate_tag("users_table").await?;
```

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-invalidation-load-race
%%| fig-cap: "The invalidation/load race."
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Reader
  participant B as Writer
  participant C as Cache
  A->>C: miss, start loader
  B->>C: invalidate tag user:42
  C-->>B: tag removed
  A->>C: store loaded old value
  C-->>A: return value
```

Generation counters are the likely Phase 1 mitigation for workloads that need stronger behavior around concurrent writes and reads.

## Source Notes

Use these files when expanding this chapter:

- [Cache invalidation note](../learning/cache-invalidation.md)
- [ReadySet reread](../../../readyset/READYSET_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Olric reread](../../../olric/OLRIC_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
